title: [CI:BUILD] Ensure no buildroot macro left in /usr/bin/docker
body:
PR: #17791 as-is will leave the buildroot macro in /usr/bin/docker in the rpmbuild process.

This commit will sanitize the buildroot macro from /usr/bin/docker.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note

string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

@rhatdan @mheon PTAL

@multimeric you can rebase on this one once merged.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

LGTM
but would like a sed heavy person to take a peak.

> I am 100% certain that `ETCDIR=%{buildroot}...` is incorrect and undesired. I've tracked it down to [a simple typo in July 2018](https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/podman/c/72adb728a4349cac42ae6ede743a70dc3771c72c?branch=main).
> 
> @lsm5 please confirm my diagnosis. If you agree, please (1) close this PR, (2) open new fedpkg MRs to fix `ETCDIR` in fedpkg.

hmm, so looks like a few things changed since that change was introduced OR I've been ignorant of it all along.

1. RE: installation step in rpmbuild process, every file that's meant to be installed should go somewhere under `%{buildroot}` . So, `ETCDIR=%{_sysconfdir}` would not work because `%{_sysconfdir} == /etc` and rpmbuild can't directly install to /etc. 

2. Having said that, I realized that podman and its subpackages install absolutely nothing to `/etc`. So, whatever path was used for ETCDIR just got ignored except for the filepath mentioned in `/usr/bin/docker`.

3. So, I can change this PR to remove the sed and change the installation step from `ETCDIR=%{buildroot}%{_sysconfdir}` to `ETCDIR=%{_sysconfdir}/containers` to preserve prior path.

How does that sound?
